rulings.Case Title: JANE FENG VS. DAVID YANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate January 29, 2014 Order Granting Defendant'S Motion For Summmary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JANE FENG's Motion To Vacate January 29, 2014 Order Granting Defendant'S Motion For Summmary Judgment. Plaintiff Jane Feng's "motion to vacate January, 29, 2014 order granting defendant's motion for summary judgment" is denied. Judgment in the case was entered in 2014, so the CCP §663a(b) jurisdictional bar applies. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ELLEN SMITH VS. WHOLE FOODS MARKET ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT WHOLE FOODS MARKET CALIFORNIA, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication. Defendant Whole Foods Market California, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff fails to show that the concrete floor where plaintiff fell constituted a dangerous condition. There is no evidence that the floor was a dangerous condition when it was in its dry, unadulterated state. Plaintiff's expert (Zachary Moore) states that the floor had an average dry slip resistance of .73, which exceeds the industry standard of .5. (Moore Dec., 7-8.) There is also no evidence that the floor was dangerously wet on the day of the occurrence. Moore assumes that the floor was wet even though there is no evidence to support that assumption. The adduced evidence shows: (1) plaintiff does not know what caused her to fall (Smith Depo., 35:23-36:1); (2) plaintiff did not see water or ice on the floor and her clothes were not wet (Smith Depo., 50:6-18); and (3) defendant's employee who examined the floor after the incident did not see anything on the floor that could have caused the slip (Douglas Dec., 2.). (See Peralta v. The Vons Companies, Inc. (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 1030 [granting summary judgment where the plaintiff did not see any liquid on the floor.]) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Whole Foods Market is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. TURO INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renotice Of Motion And The Peoples Motion To Compel Further Responses To The Peoples Second Set Of Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, ACTING BY AND THROUGH DENNIS J. HERRERA AS CITY ATTORNEY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Renotice Of To Compel Further Responses To The Peoples Second Set Of Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Julia Campins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. The People's declaration of necessity is sufficient to permit it serve the additional discovery requests. Turo's argument that it is a Personal Vehicle Sharing Program, which it admits would subject it to the requirements of Insurance Code section 11580.24, place into issue its actions in compliance with that statute, even though the People are not seeking to enforce the Insurance Code in this case. The evidence sought is potentially relevant to Turo's argument that it is not a rental car agency, but rather a Personal Vehicle Sharing Program. To the extent that Turo maintains an objection that some of the requests are unduly burdensome because of the scope of time and therefore number of documents required to be identified, the parties are directed to meet and confer regarding proposed solutions, such as sampling or otherwise, that would permit the People to assess whether it is necessary and proper to pursue further discovery on those facts. Finally, as the People concede, Turo is not required to produce information it does not have in its possession, custody, or control. The People's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to julia@campinsbenhambaker.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE S. DORFMAN VS. RICHARD CARTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Lawrence Dorfman'S Motion To Lift Stay Pending Defendants Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2019, Line 12, PLAINTIFF LAWRENCE DORFMAN's Motion To Lift Stay Pending Defendants Appeal. Plaintiff Dorfman's motion to lift stay or require undertaking is denied. First, the motion violates Code of Civ. Proc., § 1008 as Dorfman is effectively asking the court to reconsider its prior order and there is no showing of new facts, law, or circumstances. Second, as the court previously ruled, Dorfman fails to demonstrate that Code of Civ. Proc., § 917.4 applies to the court's order of partition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. ALBERT J DOWNING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2019, Line 7, PLAINTIFF DISCOVER BANK's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Grant. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BULLIVANT HOUSER BAILEY PC VS. WARREN HAVENS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of: Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT WARREN HAVENS' Re-Notice Of: Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss. Defendant Warren Havens' "motion to quash service of summons or stay or dismiss" is denied. As set out in plaintiff's papers, Havens was properly served by personal service. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ARMANDO REYES VS. STARBUCKS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Seal Top-Secret Documents Submitted Within Counselor Emily Grace Genge'S Supporting Declaration; Req. For Monetary Sanctions And A Req. For A Public Reprimand; Etc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 25, 2019, Line 9, PLAINTIFF ARMANDO REYES' Motion To Seal Top-Secret Documents Submitted Within Counselor Emily Grace Genge's Supporting Declaration; Request For Monetary Sanctions And A Request For A Public Reprimand; Etc. Plaintiff's "motion to seal top-secret documents" is denied without prejudice. The motion is not clear enough to allow the court to make the findings required by California Rule of Court 2.550(d). E.g., the motion at different points seeks sealing only of "certain portions of a single page" or only plaintiff's "date of birth," but then refers to "top-secret medical information." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
